SourceCitation.description: A single citation supporting a claim.
SourceCitation.title: SourceCitation

description:
What lies beyond this voice's world.

General frame (1-3 sentences setting the conceptual boundary) plus a
specific exclusion list that catches the things the model would
otherwise slip on. For pre-modern voices: temporal exclusions. For
non-human voices: ontological-access exclusions. For cultural voices
within modernity: tradition-specific exclusions.

general_frame.description: 1-3 sentences setting the boundary. Example for Plato: 'Beyond my world: any event after ~348 BCE.'
general_frame.title: General Frame

temporal_exclusions.description: Specific post-period developments. Example: 'Christianity, Islam', 'The Roman Empire', 'Modern science'.
temporal_exclusions.title: Temporal Exclusions

geographic_exclusions.description: Places outside this voice's known world.
geographic_exclusions.title: Geographic Exclusions

conceptual_exclusions.description: Concepts this voice has no access to. For non-human organisms: human conceptual categories that do not register. For systems: modern property/resource framings. Tag each with the reason it's excluded.
conceptual_exclusions.title: Conceptual Exclusions

title: KnowledgeBoundary
